channel.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
channel.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

image.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
image.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

item.title: Houthis announce maritime blockade on Saudi Arabia
item.description: Yemen’s Iran-backed Houthi rebels have announced a maritime blockade on Saudi Arabia after the two sides traded fire

item.title: Trump vows Iran will ‘pay’ for killing US soldiers
item.description: The US intensifies air strikes on Iran, targeting critical sites after the deaths of American soldiers in the region.

item.title: ‘Now or never’: Students defy Delhi lockdown to march on India’s parliament
item.description: Youth-led protesters answer call of Cockroach Janta Party to demand accountability, sweeping reforms after exam scandal.

item.title: Petrol prices climb as US–Iran tensions disrupt markets
item.description: US petrol prices reach $4.00 per gallon (3.78 litres), marking the second week of increased costs for consumers.

item.title: FIFA World Cup: Five key takeaways from 2026 edition won by Spain
item.description: Spain beat Argentina, but talking points remain. Should there be 48 or 64 teams? Hydration breaks? The 'dark arts'?

item.title: Mexican drug lord Ismael ‘El Mayo’ Zambada sentenced to life in US prison
item.description: US judge hands life sentence to Ismael Zambada for leading one of Mexico's most violent drug cartels for decades.

item.title: Spain team arrives home after winning the World Cup
item.description: World champions Spain have landed home carrying the World Cup trophy a day after defeating Argentina in the final.

item.title: Israeli air strike injures 14 in Gaza City
item.description: An Israeli air strike on Gaza City injured at least 14 people, according to Gaza health officials.

item.title: Philippine sailor injured during clash with Chinese coast guard
item.description: A member of China’s coast guard is accused of injuring a Philippine sailor after striking him with a wooden baton.

item.title: At least 25 killed as floods and landslides hit northern India
item.description: Monsoon rains devastate communities, with rescue and relief operations intensifying and the death toll rising.

item.title: Lebanon’s military starts ‘pilot zone’ takeover in south
item.description: Implementation of the trilateral framework agreement between Israel, Lebanon and the US is to start in three villages.

item.title: Iran says diplomatic efforts ongoing via mediators amid fighting with US
item.description: An Iranian army spokesman asserts the war will continue until deterrence against further attacks has been achieved.

item.title: Spain go back to the top of FIFA rankings, Morocco reach record high
item.description: Norway saw the biggest jump in their rankings, climbing 12 spots to 19th after reaching the World Cup quarterfinals.

item.title: Former England manager and two-time Ballon d’Or winner Keegan dies aged 75
item.description: Kevin Keegan, the iconic striker for Liverpool, Newcastle and England, was diagnosed with stage four cancer last year.

item.title: China, immigration, drugs: Behind US policy in Latin America
item.description: Latin America analyst Brian Winter argues that Trump sees Venezuela as a foreign policy success… and Cuba may be next.

item.title: Who is Khalil al-Hayya, Hamas’s newly elected political chief?
item.description: Hamas has announced the election of Khalil al-Hayya as the new head of its political bureau.

item.title: Yemen’s Houthis declare naval blockade of Saudi Arabia: What to know
item.description: Yemeni group says the announcement is based on the principle of 'an eye for an eye'.

item.title: Ebola death toll in DRC surges to at least 930 as outbreak continues spread
item.description: Ebola spreading faster than any previous outbreak in DRC, with 37 new deaths recorded over just 24 hours at the weekend.

item.title: Andy Burnham pledges end to political instability as he becomes UK PM
item.description: Britain's seventh PM in a decade pledged major changes in bid to see off populist forces that are making large gains.

item.title: What are ‘civilian’ sites in international law and where have US, Iran hit?
item.description: The US has escalated attacks on Iranian roads, bridges and power plants, and Iran too, has responded.

item.title: What we know about US soldiers killed in Iran war, and Iranian casualties
item.description: Trump has expressed sadness and vowed to hit Iran 'very hard' to honour killed US soldiers.

item.title: Petro to give farewell speech amid Colombia transfer-of-power tensions
item.description: The outgoing president has called for protests as he continues to dispute the results of last month's election.

item.title: Israeli ministers and settlers call for return to Gaza settlements
item.description: ‘Gaza is ours.’

item.title: Burnham’s move to scrap UK’s digital ID plans earns cheers and criticism
item.description: Digital inclusion and civil liberties campaigners welcome the U-turn — for different reasons. But some are unhappy.

item.title: Why Palestinians in Gaza are celebrating Spain’s World Cup win
item.description: Thousands gathered across Gaza to watch the World Cup final, with many backing Spain over its support for Palestinians.
